typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: SessionDetailView still does not clear prior conversation/summary state before the async refresh completes, so the rapid-switch stale-state leak remains possible despite the .task(id:) cancellation change.
Session Detail tests remain data-layer-only and still do not instantiate SessionDetailView or ContextPackSheet, so the contract's UI visibility/order/presentation wiring is not actually exercised.

CrossFlow tests still bypass the real Dashboard/SessionDetail/ContextPackSheet path and do not assert exact ordered session-id equality plus normalized shared-body equality, leaving same-anchor parity regressions unverified.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen the UI-worker procedure to require explicit stale-state clearing (or equivalent view-identity scoping) for reused SessionDetail content; `.task(id:)` cancellation alone is not sufficient. Also require cross-flow reviewers/tests to instantiate the real DashboardView -> SessionDetailView -> ContextPackSheet launch path and to assert ordered session-id equality plus normalized shared-body parity.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-session review still found stale conversation/summary leakage after the async refresh change, and the crossflow review found the tests still bypass real SwiftUI entrypoints and only approximate same-anchor parity.
